title: Semantic sections
description: header, nav, main, footer — meaning, not divs.

questions.prompt: How many <main> elements should a page have?
questions.explanation: Exactly one visible <main> — it's the main content landmark.

questions.prompt: Which is a semantic container for site navigation?
questions.explanation: <nav> — announces to screen readers as a navigation landmark.

questions.prompt: Replace these generic divs with the right semantic landmarks.
questions.explanation: header/nav/main/footer are recognized landmarks for assistive tech.
aside = content tangentially related to what surrounds it.

questions.prompt: For an image with a caption:
questions.explanation: figure groups the media and its caption semantically.

questions.prompt: How many <nav> elements per page are OK?
options: One per major group of links
questions.explanation: Multiple <nav>s are fine (main, footer). Label each with aria-label.
First rule of ARIA: prefer native HTML.

options: Skip repeated content like the site header
questions.explanation: Main = unique primary content of THIS page.
